description: Baseline: all 7 scripts benchmarked. json-vis=54s (BOTTLENECK), mr-plan-eval=22s, ctx-inject=503ms, read-turn=87ms, envelope=75ms, watch-agent=79ms

asi.bottleneck: json-vis at 54s for 12MB JSONL — processes line-by-line with jq calls per line
optimization_targets: mr-plan-eval (22s — mostly API wait, minimal script overhead)

description: Rewrite json-vis from bash+awk+jq to pure Python: 54s→56ms (614x faster). Identical output format verified.

asi.compatibility: Same flags (--no-thinking, --compact), same output format, verified with synthetic + real data
asi.hypothesis: Replace per-line jq fork+exec with Python json.loads
asi.mechanism: jq costs ~2.6ms per fork. 12MB JSONL triggered ~20,000 forks in the bash version. Python parses entire file in 50ms.
